prevalence.trend: stable; burden reflects smoking history and aging

burden.dalyPerCapita: moderate due to chronic respiratory disability

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Spirometry with airflow limitation and imaging findings (hyperinflation); assess COPD phenotype

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; diagnosis based on symptoms and spirometry
publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Hundreds of thousands to low millions (subset of COPD)
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; depends on smoking cessation and inhaler access

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Spirometry access in pulmonary clinics; chest imaging for emphysema pattern
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Smoking cessation, inhaled bronchodilators, pulmonary rehab, vaccinations; oxygen for advanced disease
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Underdiagnosis due to limited spirometry in primary care
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: COPD medications and pulmonary rehab available in reference centers
